title: Democracy Decided by Algorithm: How Romania Became the First Country to Cancel Elections Because of TikTok
content:
Romania is still trying to understand how it set a precedent that many analysts consider dangerous. The cancellation of the 2024 presidential elections, due to manipulation through TikTok, marked the defeat of democracy in the face of social networks, a Romanian researcher argues in a study published in March.

Credited in polls with only a few percentage points, Călin Georgescu came first in the first round of the presidential elections on November 24, 2024. A public figure previously considered marginal, Georgescu quickly transformed into a candidate supported by an overwhelming wave of messages on social media, which portrayed him almost as a messianic figure on TikTok, Facebook, YouTube, and other platforms.

Behind this
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: Equal Opportunities in Europe? A Growing Skepticism Among the Young
content:
Talent, hard work or family wealth? What really determines success today? Young Europeans grew up believing they lived on a continent of equal opportunity. Today, they increasingly feel that success depends not on skills or effort, but on their parents’ financial resources.

Europe is often portrayed as a continent of social fairness – a place where prosperity, universal public education, and integration projects were meant to level the playing field. Programs such as Erasmus+, free or low-cost higher education in many countries, and extensive welfare systems were all designed with this goal in mind.

In theory, these mechanisms were supposed to ensure that a young European – regardless of where they were born—would have a similar start
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: What the war in Iran means for Ukraine and Russia
content:
A war with Iran could influence the course of the conflict in Ukraine. Rising oil prices, limited weapons supplies, and shifting Western priorities may simultaneously strengthen Russia while complicating Kyiv’s position.

At first glance, a conflict between Iran and the United States and their allies in the Middle East should have little impact on the war that has been ongoing in Ukraine since 2022. The two are separated by thousands of kilometers, shaped by different strategic realities and driven by distinct political objectives. However, in an era of global military, energy, and financial interdependence, any major conflict acts like a tectonic shock – shifting the balance of power even in regions far removed from the battlefield.

For bot
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: More renovation and construction, less bureaucracy – that’s how the EU will tackle the housing crisis
content:
Unspent funds from the Recovery plan could be redirected toward affordable housing. However, there are concerns that lowering standards will only benefit the industry.

Mobilizing funds and other financial resources to boost housing construction and the renovation of aging and energy-inefficient buildings, along with relaxing construction regulations – these are the main measures outlined by the European Parliament after more than a year of analysis and discussion by the Special Committee on the Housing Crisis (HOUS). It tackles an issue that has become a problem for millions of Europeans: housing is scarce and unaffordable. Ultimately, it has proposed a solution, though one that has so far been met with mixed reactions.
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: EU ETS: Evolution, not revolution – Poland pushes for relief now
content:
Despite growing political pressure from Italy, Germany, and Poland it appears that the revision of the system will be evolutionary rather than revolutionary. Each side has secured part of its demands, but none has achieved everything.

During the European Parliament’s plenary debate ahead of the European Council meeting on 19–20 March 2026, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen stated that without the ETS, the European Union would be consuming around 100 billion cubic metres more gas today—resulting in significantly greater dependence on fossil fuel imports and increased exposure to geopolitical energy risks.

creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: Czech Government ‘Destroyed’ Czech Foreign Humanitarian Aid, NGOs Say
content:
Non-profit organisations have accused the Czech government of effectively dismantling the country’s foreign humanitarian aid programme after the ruling coalition slashed funding in the 2026 state budget.

The Czech humanitarian organisations that administer the aid programmes told Update-EU that the decision amounts to the “destruction” of Czech humanitarian assistance abroad.

“The Chamber of Deputies has approved a truthful, complete and realistic state budget. It delivers operational savings, lower contributions for the self-employed, higher wages and cheaper electricity, which helped bring inflation in January to its lowest level in nine years,” Czech Finance Minister Alena Schillerová (ANO) said at
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: Romania and Ukraine Plan Joint Drone Production Under New Strategic Partnership
content:
Romanian President Nicușor Dan and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy signed a Strategic Partnership declaration in Bucharest, marking a new stage in bilateral relations between the two neighboring countries.

Among the initiatives discussed is a plan to launch joint drone production in Romania, alongside agreements on defense co-production and cooperation in the energy sector.

“Mr. President, dear Volodymyr, thank you for your visit, and above all, thank you and the Ukrainian people for the fight you are waging – a fight for all of Europe, and therefore for Romania as well,” President Nicușor Dan said during a joint press conference with his Ukrainian counterpart.

creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: Bots and trolls on social networks are becoming uncontrollable: who benefits from this?
content:
When opinion leaders, journalists, or other individuals post on social media about highly topical issues, such as the possible lack of transparency of politicians, for example, Member of Parliament Remigijus Žemaitaitis and his party Nemuno aušra, the war in Ukraine, and national security issues, they almost instantly receive numerous reactions and comments from bots and trolls.

Such posts become a field of activity for coordinated inauthentic accounts, where false information is shared, artificial tension is created, discord is stirred up, social antagonism is deepened, and trust in state institutions and democratic processes is undermined.

creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: EU presents new energy package amid ongoing crisis
content:
The European Commission has announced a comprehensive strategy for the energy transition, combining investments in clean energy with support for citizens. In response to sharp increases in energy commodity prices, Brussels is focusing on accelerating electrification and reducing dependence on imported fossil fuels

Rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have exposed structural weaknesses in Europe’s energy system. Brent crude prices surpassed $100 per barrel in just three days, while European natural gas futures spiked after Qatar, the world’s largest LNG producer, halted production.

The impact on Europe’s energy costs is significant. According to experts, gas price volatility alone cost Europe €620 million within the first three da
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: Is it true that in Latvia immigrants can exchange “any little piece of paper” for a driving licence?
content:
In February, a claim by Ivars Lipskis, a user of the social media platform X, attracted considerable attention. He claimed that foreigners are “basically able to automatically exchange any little piece of paper for a Latvian driving licence”.

The Road Traffic Safety Directorate (Ceļa satiksmes drošības direkcija, CSDD) explains that such statements are not true. Lipskis’s claim that “brown people in old cars are not being bothered” is also incorrect.

CSDD representative Mārtiņš Mālmeisters reminds that Latvia has joined the 1968 Vienna Convention on Road Traffic, which establishes the recognition of driving licences. This means that people who are not citizens of the European Union (EU) may also drive a ve
creator: Olga Jurkowska
